title: Tennis Star Carlos Alcaraz Will Miss the French Open Due to a Wrist Injury
summary:
Bad news for tennis fans! Carlos Alcaraz, the 22-year-old superstar who won the French Open two years in a row, says he can't play this year. He hurt his right wrist at the Barcelona Open, and after tests, doctors told him to take a break. So he's also skipping the Italian Open and the French Open, which are both played on clay courts.

Alcaraz posted on Instagram: "This is a difficult time for me, but I am sure we will come out of this stronger." He started 2026 by winning the Australian Open, becoming the youngest man ever to win all four major tournaments (that's called a career Grand Slam). He's known for being amazing on clay—last year he won tournaments in Monte Carlo, Rome, and Roland Garros (the French Open).

His big rival, Jannik Sinner, said: "It is very sad news. Hopefully he can be back for Wimbledon." Sinner is now the world number one, and he could win the French Open himself—it's the only major title he hasn't won yet. Let's hope Alcaraz heals quickly and comes back stronger!
why_it_matters: Even top athletes get hurt sometimes, and this reminds us that taking care of our bodies is more important than competing.

Carlos Alcaraz is a Spanish tennis player who started very young and quickly became one of the best in the world.
Jannik Sinner is an Italian tennis player who is currently ranked number one in the world.
Article_Structure: WHO: Carlos Alcaraz, a 22-year-old tennis star from Spain.
WHAT: He announced he will miss the French Open because of a wrist injury.
WHERE: The French Open is played in Paris, France, on clay courts.
WHY: Doctors told him to take a break so he can heal and come back stronger for future tournaments.

perspectives.description: They are disappointed not to see him play, but they support his decision to recover.
They are curious to see who will win the French Open now, especially since Alcaraz and Sinner are both missing or unproven on clay.
They are excited because Sinner has a better chance to win the only major title he hasn’t won yet.
